channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Day burning, 29th May
item.description:
Outside 458 Bromley Road Street light column T2/106 is day burning

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 458, Bromley Road, Southend, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Playground apparatus, 29th May
item.description:
Playground apparatus needs attention. Yellow barriers are put around a faulty apparatus and then promptly scattered around the playground by older children. One of the swings badly needs greasing, as the screeching noise it makes is disturbing all the residents in the flats which are in close proximity to the playground. The grass needs more frequent mowing. We also see people taking their dogs inside the playground. There is no sign on the first gate when approaching the playground from Finucane Drive.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stanton Close, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 25cm diagram potwhole in the football field. Danger to ppl!!, 29th May
item.description:
I have been playing with my kids this Sunday and injured my right ankle in this potwhole. I have suffered a bad injury, couldn’t get up and had to get help walking home. Ruined my day and week. Putting regular ice, and deep heat cream. Now (Friday) I am managing to walk arround. My personality didnt let me sit at home but did go to work but took it easy. Same didnt go to the a&e because didnt want to waste a day there. I did go there back today to make pictures and report. I also covered the whole with a stam of wood when leaving. Please fix that as a matter of urgency to avoid more ppl falling into the same whole. As a council tax payer and pay alot I did not expect that and find the Council accountable for that. Please get in touch with me if this was reported before and how come this was not covered in time. Please share a email address that I can opload more photos of the site, and my injury. Thank you Rothtzvi@gmail.com 07889747391 Kind regards, T. Roth

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ashleys Alley, West Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Untaxed not moved for weeks, 29th May
item.description:
Car is untaxed and has been stationary for weeks potentially dumped no movement for long period of time taking valuable space in centre of car park

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20-42, Bargrove Close, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly Tipping Truman’s Road, 29th May
item.description:
More fly tipping on Truman’s Road, both in the image shown and further up the road there’s also a blue pallet that’s been disregarded on the pavement… it’s happening way too often.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Salcombe Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unknown male using residents car park to store and repair untaxed cars driving the cars around the estate, 29th May
item.description:
As per previous report same man using residents car park to fix and drive untaxed cars in a highly disabled and vulnerable area of the estate taking 2 spaces creating noise nuisance revving and driving where taxed and insured cars are parked second time he has been out there

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20-42, Bargrove Close, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Urgent Action Required: Persistent Fly-Tipping and Overflowing Bins Outside Raebarn Court now a public health risk, 29th May
item.description:
I am writing to raise a formal complaint regarding the ongoing fly-tipping and overflowing bins at the end of Gracefield Gardens, outside Raebarn Court. This situation is now completely out of control. For well over a year, residents have been forced to endure a persistent accumulation of rubbish with no effective long-term solution from the Council. Rather than improving, the problem is worsening by the day. The overflowing bins and surrounding fly-tipped waste have now attracted rats, which can regularly be seen climbing in and out of the rubbish. Given that these bins are located directly beside a chemist and a dental practice, and opposite a health centre, this has become a serious public health concern. It is unacceptable that healthcare facilities and local residents should be subjected to such unsanitary conditions. The volume of rubbish has also reached the point where the footpath is frequently obstructed. Pedestrians, including parents with buggies, wheelchair users, elderly residents and anyone with mobility issues, are often forced into the road in order to pass. This creates an obvious safety risk in addition to the hygiene issues. Lambeth Council has statutory responsibilities in relation to waste collection, environmental health and keeping highways clear of obstructions. The ongoing conditions at this location suggest that the current arrangements are failing to meet those responsibilities. Residents have reported and tolerated this problem for far too long. The current approach is clearly not working. I would therefore like to know: I would therefore like a response addressing the following points: 1. What immediate action will be taken to remove the accumulated waste and address the vermin problem? 2. What assessment has been made of the public health implications of this site? 3. What permanent measures are being introduced to prevent the bins from repeatedly overflowing and to deter fly-tipping? 4. What timescale has been set for implementing those measures? 5. Which Council department and officer are responsible for overseeing a long-term resolution? I would also request that this complaint be treated as a formal complaint under the Council’s complaints procedure and that I receive a substantive written response rather than a generic acknowledgement. After more than a year of worsening conditions, residents are entitled to expect decisive action rather than temporary clean-ups followed by a rapid return to the same situation.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2-8, Gracefield Gardens, Streatham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown tree, 29th May

Tree obstructing the highway as so overgrown

item.title: Canal requires cleaning, 29th May
item.description:
The canal behind Windrush School is in a disgusting state with weeds and rubbish. Birds are having difficulty swimming about.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rowntree Path, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unknown man using car park to store and drive untaxed cars, 29th May
item.description:
Unknown male left 2 untaxed cars in residents car park and had been using the area to try and repair cars but had been driving these cars around the estate with no tax and no care for other taxes and insured vehicles as previously reported this is a heavily disabled and vulnerable area and there are now 2 spaces less for careers and hospital transport to attend this is the 2nd time this same person had gone this he does not as far as I am aware live here

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20-42, Bargrove Close, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Gutters full of debris and litter sholden gardens / Westbere house, 29th May
item.description:
Debris and litter in gutters next to bin sholden gardens westbere house ,clarion caretaker says it's not his job ,surely in front and around westbere house it is ,needs sweeping and a litter pick ,such low low standards ,pay council tax for this !

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sholden Gardens, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Motorbike jumps on public path, 29th May
item.description:
Someone has started to make bike jumps on the main footpath just behind the Eynsford Close Rec. these are blocking the path. Fast bikes and young kids don’t mix. These need to be removed. There is enough of an issue with motorbikes in these woods without these jumps being built

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ember Close, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped or stolen motorcycles, 29th May
item.description:
One of several that have been dumped here for several years without moving. It has SORN off the road status. They are dangerous to kids on the estate who climb on them and have fallen over. Motorycles are forbidden under the lease terms of Parkside Estate.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rutland Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping on Parkside Road, 29th May
item.description:
There is a gas bottle and other rubbish dumped on Parkside Rd between Ashburnham Rd and Mayfield Rd. I have repeatedly tried to upload photos but it doesnt work
